General

5 questions
AIDLA is Pakistan's #1 education platform where you can learn, earn AIDLA Coins, and win real prizes. Complete quizzes, spin the lucky wheel, enter lucky draws, and convert your coins into cash or products.
👁 24 views🌐 General
Was this helpful?100% found helpful
Yes! AIDLA is completely free to join. Simply create an account, start learning, and earn coins immediately. No credit card or payment required.
👁 18 views🌐 General
Was this helpful?100% found helpful
AIDLA is designed for Pakistani students and learners, but anyone from anywhere can sign up. Our content is focused on the Pakistani education curriculum and local opportunities.
👁 5 views🌐 General
Was this helpful?100% found helpful
AIDLA offers several free AI tools for Pakistani students. Our platform features an AI summarizer that can help you condense long texts into shorter, more manageable summaries. We also have a CV maker that uses AI to help you create a professional-looking CV. To access these tools, simply visit https://www.aidla.online/ and navigate to the 'Tools' section. Our AI tools are designed to make your life easier and help you succeed in your studies. Check them out today and see how they can benefit you!
👁 2 views🌐 General
Was this helpful?100% found helpful

AIDLA is an online education platform designed to help Pakistani students learn and earn through various tools and resources.

AIDLA provides students with access to a range of educational tools, including calculators, planners, and career resources, to support their academic and professional development. The platform also offers courses and resources to enhance skills and knowledge.

  • Key features: AIDLA's tools include a CGPA Calculator and a CV Maker
  • Benefits: Students can improve their academic performance and increase their employability
  • Accessibility: AIDLA's resources are available online, making it accessible to students across Pakistan

By utilizing AIDLA's resources, students can effectively manage their time, enhance their skills, and increase their chances of success in their chosen careers, all while earning and learning with AIDLA.

🪙

Coins & Rewards

6 questions
You can earn AIDLA Coins by completing quizzes and tests, spinning the Lucky Wheel daily, entering and winning lucky draws, referring friends to AIDLA, and participating in special events and challenges.
👁 4 views🪙 Coins & Rewards
Was this helpful?100% found helpful
To earn money online in Pakistan, you can start by signing up on AIDLA at https://www.aidla.online/signup. On AIDLA, you can earn coins by completing quizzes, daily tasks, and activities, which can then be redeemed for cash, prizes, and vouchers. Visit the Wallet page at /user/wallet to learn more about withdrawing your coins as real cash to Easypaisa, JazzCash, or bank.
👁 1 views🪙 Coins & Rewards
Was this helpful?
Go to the Rewards Shop and browse available items. You can redeem coins for gadgets, gift cards, mobile credit, and more. A minimum coin balance is required for each reward.
👁 1 views🪙 Coins & Rewards
Was this helpful?100% found helpful
AIDLA Coins do not expire as long as your account remains active. If your account is inactive for more than 12 months, coins may be subject to expiry. We will notify you by email before any expiry.
👁 2 views🪙 Coins & Rewards
Was this helpful?100% found helpful

Inviting friends on AIDLA earns you referral bonus coins by sharing your unique referral link with them to join the platform.

To invite friends, navigate to the referrals section on AIDLA, copy your unique link, and share it with friends via social media, email, or messaging apps. When a friend joins using your link, you both receive bonus coins.

  • Referral link: Find your unique link in the AIDLA referrals section
  • Sharing options: Share your link on social media, via email, or through messaging apps
  • Coin rewards: Earn bonus coins for each successful referral

You can track your referral progress and bonus coins using your AIDLA account, and consider using the free AIDLA Grade Calculator to stay on top of your studies.

👁 0 views🪙 Coins & Rewards
Was this helpful?

Your AIDLA leaderboard rank is determined by the points you earn from completing courses, achieving milestones, and engaging with the platform.

To check your rank, log in to your AIDLA account, navigate to the leaderboard section, and view your current standing among other students. Climbing higher requires consistent effort, such as completing daily study tasks and participating in quizzes.

  • Earning points: Focus on completing courses and achieving milestones to maximize your points
  • Consistency: Regularly log in and engage with the platform to maintain your momentum
  • Competition: Compare your progress with others on the leaderboard to stay motivated
  • Goal setting: Use the AIDLA Study Planner to set realistic goals and track your progress

Utilize AIDLA tools to streamline your learning and stay ahead on the leaderboard.

Tests & Quizzes

3 questions
AIDLA offers tests in Mathematics, English, Urdu, Science, Social Studies, Islamiyat, Computer Science, and more — aligned with the Pakistani curriculum from Grade 1 to Intermediate level.
👁 5 views📝 Tests & Quizzes
Was this helpful?100% found helpful
Yes, most quizzes can be retaken. However, coins are only awarded for your first attempt or when you improve your previous score. Retaking helps you practice and improve your ranking on the leaderboard.
👁 1 views📝 Tests & Quizzes
Was this helpful?

To take quizzes on AIDLA and earn coins, log in to your account, navigate to the quizzes section, and select a quiz to start.

Each quiz consists of multiple-choice questions, and you earn coins for every correct answer. The number of coins awarded depends on the difficulty level and category of the quiz.

  • Quiz categories: AIDLA offers quizzes in various subjects, including mathematics, science, and English
  • Coin rewards: Correct answers earn you coins, which can be redeemed for rewards or used to access premium content
  • Leaderboards: Compete with other students to achieve the highest scores and rank on the leaderboards

You can track your progress and plan your study schedule using the free AIDLA Study Planner.

👁 0 views📝 Tests & Quizzes
Was this helpful?

Lucky Draw

3 questions
Lucky Draws are prize competitions you enter using your AIDLA Coins. Each draw has a specific prize and entry cost in coins. Winners are selected randomly and announced on the platform. All draws are fair and transparent.
👁 2 views🎲 Lucky Draw
Was this helpful?100% found helpful
Winners are selected using a verified random selection process. All entries have an equal chance of winning regardless of how many coins were spent on entry.
👁 2 views🎲 Lucky Draw
Was this helpful?100% found helpful

AIDLA lucky wheel is a gamified rewards system where you can spin a virtual wheel to win prizes such as scholarships, premium courses, and career resources.

To participate, you need to create an account on AIDLA and earn spin tokens by completing specific tasks, such as taking quizzes or referring friends. Each spin gives you a chance to win a prize.

  • Prize categories: Scholarships, premium courses, and career resources are some of the prizes you can win
  • Earning spin tokens: Complete tasks such as quizzes, referrals, or achieving milestones to earn tokens
  • Winning chances: Each spin is independent, and the probability of winning remains the same

You can explore available prizes and check your eligibility using the AIDLA Scholarship Checker.

Withdrawals

4 questions
Go to your wallet, tap Withdraw, and enter your bank account or JazzCash/EasyPaisa details. A minimum withdrawal amount applies. Withdrawals are processed within 3-5 working days.
👁 1 views💵 Withdrawals
Was this helpful?100% found helpful
The minimum withdrawal amount is 500 AIDLA Coins equivalent in PKR. The exact PKR conversion rate is shown in your wallet at the time of withdrawal.
👁 1 views💵 Withdrawals
Was this helpful?100% found helpful
To withdraw your AIDLA coins, head over to https://www.aidla.online/user/wallet. On this page, you'll see your total coin balance and a 'Withdraw' button. Click on it to initiate the withdrawal process. You'll need to link your Easypaisa or JazzCash account to your AIDLA account to receive the cash. Follow the on-screen instructions to complete the withdrawal. Make sure you have enough coins to meet the minimum withdrawal requirement. If you have any issues, feel free to reach out to us for help. You can also check your wallet page for more information on withdrawals and transfers.
👁 0 views💵 Withdrawals
Was this helpful?
When you withdraw your coins from AIDLA, it usually takes 2-3 working days to reach your bank account. First, make sure you have enough coins in your wallet, which you can check at https://www.aidla.online/user/wallet. To withdraw, simply click on 'Withdraw' and follow the steps. AIDLA processes withdrawals quickly, but bank processing times can vary. If you have any issues, feel free to reach out to us for help.

Was this helpful?

Education

15 questions
In Pakistan's matric system (Grade 9-10), students are graded as: A1 (80-100%), A (70-79%), B (60-69%), C (50-59%), D (40-49%), and below 40% is a fail. The board exams are conducted by regional Boards of Intermediate and Secondary Education (BISE).
👁 1 views🎓 Education
Was this helpful?100% found helpful
To create a CV for free, you can use AIDLA's CV Maker tool. It offers 20+ templates and allows you to export your CV as a PDF. Simply visit https://www.aidla.online/tools/career/cv-maker, choose a template, fill in your details, and download your CV. No sign-up is required, and it's completely free.
👁 11 views🎓 Education
Was this helpful?100% found helpful
These are intermediate (Grade 11-12) program types in Pakistan. FA (Faculty of Arts) covers humanities. FSc (Faculty of Science) covers pre-medical and pre-engineering. ICS (Intermediate in Computer Science) covers computing. ICom (Intermediate in Commerce) covers business and accounting.
👁 4 views🎓 Education
Was this helpful?100% found helpful
Major scholarships in Pakistan include HEC Need-Based Scholarships, Ehsaas Scholarship, PEEF Scholarships, and various provincial scholarships. Apply through the HEC portal (hec.gov.pk) or your institution's financial aid office. Requirements typically include academic merit, family income proof, and domicile certificate.
👁 3 views🎓 Education
Was this helpful?100% found helpful
The AIDLA Free Courses Portal is a learning section on the AIDLA platform where users can access educational courses and develop new skills online completely free. Users simply need to sign up, log in, and navigate to Dashboard → Courses to start learning.
👁 4 views🎓 Education
Was this helpful?100% found helpful
To start learning on AIDLA, visit https://www.aidla.online, create a free account or log in, go to your Dashboard, and open the Courses section. From there, you can browse available courses and begin learning instantly without any payment.
👁 3 views🎓 Education
Was this helpful?100% found helpful
To calculate your CGPA and GPA, visit https://www.aidla.online/tools/cgpa-calculator. This free tool is designed specifically for Pakistani students. Simply enter your grades for each subject, and the calculator will do the rest. You can use this calculator for any semester or academic year. Follow the on-screen instructions to get your CGPA and GPA in seconds. AIDLA's calculator is accurate and reliable, making it a great resource for students. For more free tools like the CGPA calculator, explore https://www.aidla.online/tools.
👁 2 views🎓 Education
Was this helpful?
To check your eligibility for HEC scholarships on AIDLA, visit our courses page at https://www.aidla.online/user/courses. We have a dedicated section for HEC scholarships where you can find all the details. Our AI bot can also guide you through the process. Simply click on the HEC scholarship section and follow the prompts to enter your details. You can then check your eligibility and get more information on how to apply.
👁 3 views🎓 Education
Was this helpful?
To check your Pakistan board results on AIDLA, head over to https://www.aidla.online/board-results. This page allows you to view results for all 26 BISE boards in Pakistan. Simply select your board, enter your roll number, and click 'Get Result' to view your scores. AIDLA makes it easy to access your board results in one convenient place. If you have any questions or need help, don't hesitate to reach out to our support team.
👁 2 views🎓 Education
Was this helpful?

Free study materials for Pakistani students can be downloaded from various online platforms, including AIDLA Resources and educational websites.

Pakistani students can access a wide range of study materials, such as textbooks, notes, and past papers, to aid in their academic preparation. Many websites offer free downloads of these materials, which can be easily accessed through a simple search.

  • Online libraries: Utilize online libraries and educational platforms that offer free study materials
  • Open-source textbooks: Leverage open-source textbooks and academic resources available for download
  • Student forums: Join student forums and discussion groups to share and download study materials

You can also visit the AIDLA Resources page to find relevant study materials and tools to support your academic journey.

👁 0 views🎓 Education
Was this helpful?

Tracking attendance regularly helps avoid shortage by ensuring you meet the 75% attendance requirement in Pakistani colleges.

To track attendance, review your institution's attendance policy, monitor your daily attendance record, and plan ahead to avoid unnecessary absences.

  • Regular monitoring: Check your attendance record regularly to identify potential shortages
  • Attendance policies: Familiarize yourself with your institution's attendance and shortage policies
  • Time management: Plan your schedule to ensure you can attend all classes and avoid unnecessary absences

You can use the free AIDLA Attendance Calculator to calculate your attendance percentage and plan accordingly.

👁 0 views🎓 Education
Was this helpful?

Failing a Pakistani board exam requires a strategic approach to improve grades, starting with re-examining the failed subjects and identifying areas of weakness.

To improve, focus on understanding the exam format, practicing with past papers, and seeking help from teachers or tutors to address knowledge gaps. Consistent effort and a study plan are essential to achieving better grades.

  • Analyse performance: Identify the subjects and topics where you need improvement
  • Seek help: Consult with teachers or tutors to understand difficult concepts
  • Practice consistently: Regular practice with past papers and sample questions helps build confidence

You can plan your studies effectively and track your progress using the free AIDLA Study Planner and assess your grades with the AIDLA Grade Calculator.

👁 0 views🎓 Education
Was this helpful?

The Pomodoro technique is a time management method that involves studying in focused 25-minute increments, called Pomodoros, separated by 5-minute breaks.

This technique helps you stay focused and avoid burnout by providing regular breaks and promoting consistent study sessions. To use the Pomodoro technique, choose a task you want to work on, set a timer for 25 minutes, and work on the task without any interruptions until the timer goes off.

  • Choose a task: Select a specific topic or subject you want to study
  • Set the timer: Use a traditional timer or a tool like the AIDLA Pomodoro Timer to keep yourself on track
  • Take breaks: Take a 5-minute break after each Pomodoro session to recharge

By incorporating the Pomodoro technique into your study routine, you can boost your productivity and achieve your academic goals more efficiently.

👁 0 views🎓 Education
Was this helpful?
To create an ATS-friendly resume/CV for free, you can use AIDLA's CV/Resume Maker tool. This tool offers 20+ templates and allows you to export your resume in PDF format without requiring sign-up. You can access it at https://www.aidla.online/tools/career/cv-maker. Simply choose a template, fill in your details, and download your resume.
👁 0 views🎓 Education
Was this helpful?
To create a cover letter for free, you can use AIDLA's Cover Letter Maker tool. This tool offers 4 templates and allows you to adjust the tone to fit your needs. You can access it at https://www.aidla.online/tools/career/cover-letter-maker. Simply fill in the required information, and the tool will generate a cover letter for you. Alternatively, AIDLA also has an AI Cover Letter tool at https://www.aidla.online/tools/ai/cover-letter that can help you generate a tailored cover letter.

Was this helpful?100% found helpful
💼

Career

6 questions

Upwork, Fiverr, and Freelancer are the best freelancing platforms for Pakistani students, offering a range of projects and flexible working hours.

These platforms provide opportunities for students to develop their skills, gain experience, and earn money while studying. You can choose from various categories, including writing, graphic design, programming, and more.

  • Popular categories: Content writing, virtual assistance, and social media management are in high demand
  • Payment terms: Most platforms offer secure payment systems, ensuring timely payments to freelancers
  • Skills development: Freelancing helps students develop valuable skills, enhancing their employability after graduation

To create a strong profile and increase your chances of getting hired, use the AIDLA CV Maker to build a professional CV.

👁 0 views💼 Career
Was this helpful?

The most in demand skills for jobs in Pakistan include digital marketing, data analysis, and programming skills such as Python and JavaScript.

In today's job market, Pakistani employers prioritize candidates with expertise in emerging technologies like artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and cloud computing. You can acquire these skills through various online courses and certification programs.

  • Digital literacy: Proficiency in Microsoft Office and Google Workspace is essential for most office jobs
  • Technical skills: Programming languages like Java, Python, and C++ are in high demand across various industries
  • Soft skills: Effective communication, teamwork, and problem-solving skills are highly valued by Pakistani employers

You can create a strong CV highlighting your skills using the free AIDLA CV Maker.

👁 0 views💼 Career
Was this helpful?

Building a portfolio for freelancing as a Pakistani student involves showcasing your skills and projects to attract potential clients.

To start, identify your areas of expertise and create a professional online presence, such as a personal website or profile on freelancing platforms. Share your projects, including academic assignments, personal initiatives, or volunteer work, to demonstrate your capabilities.

  • Highlight transferable skills: Emphasize skills relevant to freelancing, such as communication, problem-solving, or time management
  • Include certifications and courses: Add any relevant certifications, training, or online courses to enhance your portfolio
  • Request feedback and testimonials: Ask clients, mentors, or peers to provide feedback and testimonials to build credibility

You can create a professional CV and portfolio using the AIDLA CV Maker to increase your chances of success in the freelancing market.

👁 0 views💼 Career
Was this helpful?

Research the company and practice common interview questions to prepare for a job interview in Pakistan.

To increase your chances of success, review the job description, and be ready to discuss your skills and experiences. Prepare any materials you may need, such as copies of your CV and references.

  • Review your CV: Make sure it is up-to-date and tailored to the job you are applying for
  • Practice your responses: Use online resources or practice with a friend to improve your confidence and communication skills
  • Use online tools: Utilize tools like the AIDLA Interview Prep to help you prepare for common interview questions

You can also use the AIDLA CV Maker to create a professional CV and increase your chances of getting hired.

👁 0 views💼 Career
Was this helpful?

A cover letter for a job application in Pakistan should introduce yourself, highlight relevant skills, and express interest in the position.

When writing a cover letter, tailor it to the specific job and company, addressing the hiring manager by name if possible, and keep it concise, ideally one page in length. Use a professional tone and format, with a formal greeting and closing.

  • Key elements: Contact information, job title, and a brief summary of qualifications
  • Customization: Research the company and incorporate relevant keywords from the job description
  • Professional tone: Use formal language throughout, avoiding slang and jargon

You can also utilize the AIDLA Cover Letter AI tool to help craft a well-structured cover letter for your job application in Pakistan.

👁 0 views💼 Career
Was this helpful?

A professional LinkedIn profile is created by showcasing relevant skills, experience, and education, making it visible to Pakistani employers.

To attract Pakistani employers, ensure your LinkedIn profile is complete, up-to-date, and highlights your achievements, certifications, and relevant coursework. Utilize keywords from your industry to improve visibility in searches.

  • Profile completeness: Ensure all sections, including education and experience, are filled out
  • Keyword optimization: Use industry-specific keywords to increase search visibility
  • Networking: Connect with professionals in your industry and engage with their content

You can create a compelling LinkedIn profile using the free AIDLA CV Maker and get ready to attract Pakistani employers.

👁 0 views💼 Career
Was this helpful?
💰

Finance

4 questions

Sending money abroad from Pakistan can be done through official banking channels or licensed money exchange companies, ensuring a legal and cheap transfer.

You can use services like online money transfers, bank drafts, or wire transfers, which are available at most banks and exchange companies in Pakistan. The State Bank of Pakistan regulates these services to ensure they comply with anti-money laundering laws.

  • Compare rates: Research and compare the exchange rates and fees charged by different banks and money exchange companies
  • Choose a reliable service: Select a reputable and licensed service to avoid any potential risks or scams
  • Check regulations: Familiarize yourself with the regulations and limits set by the State Bank of Pakistan for international money transfers

You can also use the free AIDLA Salary Calculator to plan your finances before making an international money transfer.

👁 0 views💰 Finance
Was this helpful?

As a student in Pakistan, saving money can be achieved by creating a budget, reducing unnecessary expenses, and taking advantage of student discounts.

Practical tips include tracking your income and expenses, cooking your own meals, and using public transport. You can also save money by buying second-hand books and using free online resources for studying.

  • Track your expenses: Keep a record of your daily expenses to identify areas where you can cut back
  • Use student discounts: Many businesses offer discounts to students, so always ask if one is available
  • Save on food: Cooking your own meals can save you a significant amount of money in the long run

For a better understanding of managing your finances, you can use the free AIDLA Salary Calculator to plan your expenses and savings.

👁 0 views💰 Finance
Was this helpful?

Conventional banking in Pakistan operates on interest-based transactions, whereas Islamic banking adheres to Shariah law, prohibiting interest and focusing on risk-sharing and fairness.

In Islamic banking, financial transactions are based on principles of mudarabah, musharakah, and murabahah, ensuring that investments are made in accordance with Islamic values. Conventional banks, on the other hand, offer a wide range of financial products and services based on interest rates.

  • Key principle: Islamic banking avoids riba, or interest, and emphasizes ethical investments
  • Financial products: Conventional banks offer a broader range of financial products, including personal loans and credit cards
  • Risk management: Islamic banks manage risk through shared responsibility and mutual guarantee

You can calculate your salary after considering the differences between conventional and Islamic banking using the free AIDLA Salary Calculator.

👁 0 views💰 Finance
Was this helpful?

Sending money abroad from Pakistan can be done through official banking channels or money transfer services, ensuring a legal and relatively cheap process.

To initiate the transfer, you will need to provide identification, the recipient's details, and choose a transfer method, such as online banking or a money transfer service like Western Union.

  • Bank Transfers: Most Pakistani banks offer international money transfer services with competitive exchange rates
  • Money Transfer Services: Companies specialize in international money transfers, often with lower fees than traditional banks
  • Exchange Rates: Compare rates among different service providers to get the best value for your money
  • Fees and Charges: Be aware of any additional fees or charges associated with the transfer

You can estimate the cost of sending money abroad and calculate the exchange rates using the free AIDLA Salary Calculator or explore more financial tools on AIDLA finance tools.

👁 0 views💰 Finance
Was this helpful?
🏥

Health

1 question

Students need 7-9 hours of sleep for better grades and optimal academic performance.

Sleep plays a crucial role in memory consolidation, cognitive function, and mental health. During sleep, your brain processes and retains information learned during the day, making it essential for learning and memory.

  • Recommended sleep duration: 7-9 hours for teenagers and young adults
  • Sleep deprivation effects: impaired cognitive function, decreased concentration, and lower grades
  • Establishing a routine: consistent sleep schedule helps regulate sleep patterns and improve academic performance

You can prioritize your sleep schedule and academic goals using the free AIDLA Study Planner.

👁 0 views🏥 Health
Was this helpful?
🏅

Scholarships

6 questions

Fully funded scholarships for Pakistani students abroad cover tuition fees, living expenses, and travel costs.

These scholarships are offered by governments, universities, and organizations to support international students, including Pakistanis, in pursuing higher education abroad. Eligibility criteria and application processes vary depending on the scholarship and institution.

  • Popular options: Fulbright, Chevening, and Commonwealth Scholarships are highly competitive and fully funded
  • Eligibility criteria: Academic merit, language proficiency, and sometimes a minimum CGPA requirement
  • Application process: Typically involves submitting transcripts, letters of recommendation, and a personal statement

You can check your eligibility for various scholarships and calculate your CGPA using the free AIDLA Scholarship Checker and CGPA Calculator.

👁 0 views🏅 Scholarships
Was this helpful?

A winning scholarship application essay is written by showcasing academic achievements, career goals, and community service in a clear, concise narrative.

To increase your chances of securing a scholarship, tailor your essay to the specific scholarship requirements, demonstrating how your skills and experiences align with the scholarship criteria. Ensure your essay is well-structured, with a strong introduction, body, and conclusion.

  • Start early: Give yourself ample time to research, outline, and draft your essay
  • Be authentic: Share your personal story, highlighting your passions and motivations
  • Use active voice: Write in an engaging, active voice to convey confidence and enthusiasm

Utilize the AIDLA AI Summarizer to refine your essay and make a lasting impression on the scholarship committee.

👁 0 views🏅 Scholarships
Was this helpful?

Turkish government scholarship applications from Pakistan are submitted through the Turkish Embassy or online portal, requiring transcripts, letters of recommendation, and language proficiency certificates.

To apply, you must meet the eligibility criteria, which includes a strong academic record and proficiency in English or Turkish. You should research the available programs and deadlines, then submit your application with all required documents.

  • Eligibility criteria: Academic excellence, language proficiency, and age limits apply
  • Required documents: Transcripts, diplomas, test scores, and letters of recommendation are necessary
  • Language proficiency: English or Turkish language skills are required for most programs
  • Scholarship benefits: Tuition fees, living expenses, and travel costs are covered for successful applicants

You can check your eligibility for various scholarships, including the Turkish government scholarship, using the AIDLA Scholarship Checker.

👁 0 views🏅 Scholarships
Was this helpful?

Scholarships for Pakistani students to study in China are offered by the Chinese government and universities, covering tuition fees and living expenses.

To be eligible, you typically need to meet specific academic requirements, such as a strong CGPA, and language proficiency in English or Chinese. You should research and apply for scholarships through the Chinese government's official website or the website of your desired university.

  • Academic requirements: A minimum CGPA of 3.0 is often required
  • Language proficiency: English or Chinese language skills are necessary
  • Application process: Submit applications through official government or university websites
  • Deadline: Check for deadlines, as they vary by scholarship and university

You can check your eligibility for scholarships and calculate your CGPA using the free AIDLA Scholarship Checker and AIDLA CGPA Calculator.

👁 0 views🏅 Scholarships
Was this helpful?

Scholarships for Pakistani girls in STEM fields include Fulbright, HEC, and USAID Merit and Needs-Based Scholarships.

These programs aim to promote women's participation in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics education. Eligibility criteria and application deadlines vary, but generally require a strong academic record and commitment to pursuing a career in a STEM field.

  • Merit-based scholarships: Awarded to students with outstanding academic performance
  • Needs-based scholarships: Provided to students who demonstrate financial need
  • Field-specific scholarships: Available for students pursuing specific STEM fields, such as engineering or computer science

You can check your eligibility for these scholarships and explore other opportunities using the AIDLA Scholarship Checker.

👁 0 views🏅 Scholarships
Was this helpful?

Chevening Scholarship is a fully-funded UK government scholarship for master's studies, available to Pakistani students with 2-3 years of work experience.

To apply from Pakistan, you must submit an online application, including a personal statement, education documents, and references. You can choose from over 150 UK universities and 12,000 master's courses.

  • Eligibility criteria: 2-3 years of work experience, a bachelor's degree, and English language proficiency
  • Application process: Online application, followed by interviews with the British Embassy or High Commission
  • Deadline: Usually in November each year, with courses starting the following September

You can check your eligibility for the Chevening Scholarship and other opportunities using the AIDLA Scholarship Checker.

👁 0 views🏅 Scholarships
Was this helpful?
📋

Pakistan Boards

11 questions

FBISE (Federal Board of Intermediate and Secondary Education) and provincial boards in Pakistan differ in their jurisdiction and examination systems.

FBISE conducts exams for students in federal territories, while provincial boards govern education within their respective provinces. Each board has its own curriculum, examination format, and grading system.

  • Jurisdiction: FBISE covers federal areas, such as Islamabad, while provincial boards cover their respective provinces
  • Curriculum: FBISE follows a federally-approved curriculum, whereas provincial boards may have variations
  • Examination format: Both FBISE and provincial boards conduct annual exams, but the format and grading may differ

To check your results or learn more about the education system in Pakistan, visit the AIDLA Board Results page.

👁 0 views📋 Pakistan Boards
Was this helpful?

You can apply for rechecking of papers in BISE Pakistan by submitting an application form within the specified timeframe, usually 15 days after result announcement.

The application form can be obtained from the BISE website or office, and you need to fill it out carefully, providing required details such as roll number, exam session, and paper codes. You will also need to pay a rechecking fee, which varies by board.

  • Required documents: Attach a copy of your result card and a bank draft for the rechecking fee
  • Timeframe: Submit your application within the specified deadline to avoid late fees
  • Result update: Check the BISE website for updates on your rechecking result, usually available after a few weeks

For more information on exam results, visit the AIDLA Board Results page.

👁 0 views📋 Pakistan Boards
Was this helpful?

You can check your Matric result online from BISE Lahore by visiting the official BISE Lahore website and entering your roll number.

BISE Lahore typically announces the Matric results on its website, and you can access your result by following the steps provided on the website. You will need to enter your roll number and other required information to view your result.

  • Required information: Roll number and exam session
  • Result availability: Results are usually available on the BISE Lahore website after the official announcement
  • Alternative method: You can also check your result through the AIDLA Board Results tool for convenience

You can visit the AIDLA Board Results page for more information on checking your Matric result online from BISE Lahore.

👁 0 views📋 Pakistan Boards
Was this helpful?

You can check FSc Part 1 and Part 2 results from BISE boards by visiting the official website of your respective board.

Each BISE board in Pakistan, such as BISE Lahore or BISE Karachi, uploads the results on their website after the official announcement. You can search for your result by entering your roll number and selecting your exam type.

  • Result availability: Results are typically available a few months after the exams
  • Required information: You need your roll number to check your result
  • Result calculation: You can calculate your percentage using the AIDLA Percentage Calculator
  • Next steps: After checking your result, you can plan your next academic steps using the AIDLA Study Planner

Remember to check the official BISE board website for the most accurate and up-to-date results.

👁 0 views📋 Pakistan Boards
Was this helpful?

The syllabus difference between old and new scheme in Pakistan lies in the revised curriculum, which includes updated course content and assessment patterns.

In the new scheme, the curriculum is more focused on practical knowledge and skills, whereas the old scheme emphasized theoretical aspects. The new scheme also includes additional subjects and electives to provide students with more options.

  • Revised curriculum: The new scheme has a more comprehensive and modern curriculum
  • Assessment patterns: The new scheme includes more continuous assessments and project-based evaluations
  • Elective subjects: The new scheme offers a wider range of elective subjects to choose from

You can explore the detailed syllabus and calculate your grades using the free AIDLA Grade Calculator.

👁 0 views📋 Pakistan Boards
Was this helpful?

FSc marks are calculated by combining the marks of four subjects, including three electives and English, with a total of 1100 marks.

In Pakistani board exams, each subject carries a certain weightage — for example, English is compulsory and has a fixed weightage, while the other three subjects can be chosen from a list of electives. Your FSc percentage is calculated by dividing the total marks obtained by the total maximum marks and multiplying by 100.

  • Compulsory subjects: English is mandatory for all students
  • Elective subjects: Students can choose three electives from a list of subjects, such as Mathematics, Biology, or Chemistry
  • Maximum marks: The total maximum marks for FSc exams is 1100
  • Percentage calculation: You can calculate your FSc percentage using the free AIDLA Percentage Calculator

You can calculate your exact FSc percentage using the free AIDLA tools.

👁 0 views📋 Pakistan Boards
Was this helpful?

FSc stands for Faculty of Science in Pakistan.

In Pakistan, FSc is a two-year post-secondary education program that students typically pursue after completing their matriculation. The program is designed to provide students with a strong foundation in science subjects, preparing them for higher education and careers in fields like medicine, engineering, and technology.

  • Pre-medical and pre-engineering streams: FSc programs are often categorized into these two main streams
  • Core subjects: Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Mathematics, and English are common subjects in FSc programs
  • Admission requirements: Students typically need to secure a minimum grade in their matriculation exams to be eligible for FSc programs

You can calculate your FSc grades and percentages using the free AIDLA Percentage Calculator.

👁 0 views📋 Pakistan Boards
Was this helpful?

BISE Rawalpindi result can be checked online by visiting the official BISE Rawalpindi website and entering your roll number.

To check your result, navigate to the results section, select your exam type, and enter your roll number. You can also check your result via SMS by sending your roll number to the designated code.

  • Required information: Roll number and exam type
  • Result availability: Results are typically available on the official website after the official announcement
  • Alternative method: Check your result via SMS or through the BISE Rawalpindi mobile app

You can also check your results and calculate your grades using the free tools available on AIDLA Board Results page.

👁 0 views📋 Pakistan Boards
Was this helpful?

Matric and O-levels are two different secondary education systems in Pakistan, with Matric being the local Pakistani curriculum and O-levels being the international British curriculum.

In Pakistan, Matric is administered by local boards, while O-levels are administered by the British Council or Cambridge International Examinations. The curriculum, assessment, and recognition of these two systems differ significantly.

  • Curriculum: Matric follows the Pakistani curriculum, while O-levels follow the British curriculum
  • Recognition: Matric is recognized locally, while O-levels are recognized internationally
  • Assessment: Matric has a more theoretical approach, while O-levels have a more practical approach

You can check your Matric or O-levels results using the free AIDLA Board Results tool.

👁 0 views📋 Pakistan Boards
Was this helpful?

The grading system in Pakistani board exams is based on a letter grade scale, where A1, A, B, and C represent different levels of achievement.

In this system, A1 is the highest grade, followed by A, B, and C, with each grade corresponding to a specific percentage range. The exact percentage range for each grade may vary depending on the board and the subject.

  • Grade A1: typically represents 80-100% marks
  • Grade A: typically represents 70-79% marks
  • Grade B: typically represents 60-69% marks
  • Grade C: typically represents 50-59% marks

You can calculate your percentage and corresponding grade using the free AIDLA Percentage Calculator or AIDLA Grade Calculator.

👁 0 views📋 Pakistan Boards
Was this helpful?

Compartment exam in Pakistan is a supplementary exam for students who failed in one or two subjects in their board exams.

To apply, students need to fill out a form provided by their respective board of education, pay the required fee, and submit it within the given deadline. The compartment exam is usually held a few months after the announcement of the regular exam results.

  • Eligibility: Students who have failed in only one or two subjects are eligible to appear in the compartment exam
  • Application process: Students can obtain the application form from their school or download it from the official website of their board of education
  • Fee structure: The fee for the compartment exam varies depending on the board of education

You can check your board results and plan your studies accordingly using the resources available on AIDLA.

👁 0 views📋 Pakistan Boards
Was this helpful?
🏛️

Admissions

12 questions

Admission to NUST without an engineering entry test is possible through SAT or ACT scores, or by applying to non-engineering programs.

NUST considers international test scores for admission to certain programs, and some programs do not require engineering entry tests. You should check the specific requirements for your desired program.

  • Eligibility criteria: Check the NUST website for program-specific requirements
  • Test scores: SAT or ACT scores may be accepted for some programs
  • Non-engineering programs: Consider applying to programs like business, social sciences, or natural sciences

You can review the eligibility criteria and calculate your chances using the AIDLA Percentage Calculator to determine your standing.

👁 0 views🏛️ Admissions
Was this helpful?

University admission in Pakistan requires documents such as matriculation and intermediate certificates, CNIC, and entrance test results.

To apply for university admission, you typically need to submit your academic transcripts, character certificate, and proof of identity. Admission requirements may vary depending on the institution and program.

  • Academic documents: Matric and intermediate certificates, diplomas, or equivalent qualifications
  • Identity proof: CNIC, B-form, or passport
  • Entrance test results: MDCAT, ECAT, or other relevant test scores
  • Additional requirements: Some universities may require additional documents, such as a medical certificate or a certificate of extracurricular activities

You can prepare for your entrance tests using the free AIDLA MDCAT/ECAT Calculator and check the required documents on the university's website.

👁 0 views🏛️ Admissions
Was this helpful?

Admission to computer science in Pakistan's top universities requires a strong intermediate or A-level degree with a minimum 60% score, along with a good score in the entry test.

Top universities such as NUST, LUMS, and GIKI consider applicants with excellent academic records and high entry test scores. You must prepare well for the entry test, which typically includes subjects like mathematics, physics, and computer science.

  • Entry test preparation: Focus on mathematics, physics, and computer science to perform well in the test
  • Academic performance: Maintain a high CGPA in your intermediate or A-level degree
  • University-specific requirements: Check the specific admission requirements for each university you are applying to

You can calculate your CGPA using the free AIDLA CGPA Calculator to ensure you meet the minimum requirements.

👁 0 views🏛️ Admissions
Was this helpful?

FAST NUCES admission requires applicants to pass the National Admission Test (NAT) or Scholastic Aptitude Test (SAT), with a minimum score of 540 in SAT-I and 1500 in SAT-II for undergraduate programs.

Applicants must submit their applications online, providing required documents such as academic transcripts and test scores. The entry test is a crucial component of the admission process, assessing a student's knowledge in subjects like mathematics, physics, and English.

  • Eligibility criteria: Applicants must have at least 60% marks in their intermediate or equivalent exams
  • Test format: The NAT test consists of multiple-choice questions, while the SAT is a paper-based test
  • Preparation: Applicants can prepare for the entry test using study resources and practice tests

You can prepare for the entry test and assess your performance using the free AIDLA MDCAT/ECAT Calculator.

👁 0 views🏛️ Admissions
Was this helpful?

Preparation for the UET Lahore entry test for engineering involves studying key subjects like Mathematics, Physics, and English, and practicing with sample papers and past exams.

The University of Engineering and Technology (UET) Lahore entry test, also known as the ECAT, assesses your knowledge in these areas, so focusing on conceptual understanding and problem-solving skills is crucial.

  • Subject focus: Mathematics, Physics, and English are the primary subjects tested
  • Practice materials: Use official sample papers, past exams, and online resources to practice
  • Time management: Develop a study plan and manage your time effectively to cover all subjects

You can use the free AIDLA MDCAT/ECAT Calculator to estimate your scores and plan your preparation strategy.

👁 0 views🏛️ Admissions
Was this helpful?

NTS GAT preparation involves studying key subjects like English, mathematics, and analytical reasoning to achieve a high score.

In Pakistan, the NTS GAT test is a crucial requirement for university admission, particularly for MS and PhD programs. You should focus on developing your problem-solving skills and improving your vocabulary.

  • Understand the test format: Familiarize yourself with the test pattern, question types, and time allocation
  • Practice with sample tests: Utilize online resources and practice tests to assess your knowledge and identify areas for improvement
  • Develop a study plan: Create a schedule and stick to it, ensuring you cover all necessary topics and revise regularly

You can create an effective study schedule using the free AIDLA Study Planner to help you prepare for the NTS GAT test.

👁 0 views🏛️ Admissions
Was this helpful?

The top 10 universities in Pakistan by HEC ranking include Quaid-i-Azam University, National University of Sciences and Technology, and Lahore University of Management Sciences.

These rankings are based on various criteria such as research output, faculty quality, and student selectivity. The Higher Education Commission (HEC) of Pakistan releases these rankings annually to provide a comprehensive overview of the country's higher education landscape.

  • Criteria: HEC rankings consider factors like faculty, research, and infrastructure
  • Frequency: Rankings are updated annually to reflect changes in university performance
  • Importance: HEC rankings influence student choice and employer perceptions of university graduates

You can explore more about these universities and their programs using the AIDLA Resources and plan your study schedule with the AIDLA Study Planner.

👁 0 views🏛️ Admissions
Was this helpful?

MDCAT test comprises four subjects: Biology, Chemistry, Physics, and English, with 88, 58, 44, and 30 questions respectively.

The MDCAT test is a multiple-choice questions based exam, and each subject carries a specific number of questions. You should prepare thoroughly for each subject to achieve a high score.

  • Biology: 88 questions, focusing on concepts and problem-solving skills
  • Chemistry: 58 questions, covering theoretical and practical aspects
  • Physics: 44 questions, emphasizing numerical problem-solving and concepts
  • English: 30 questions, testing language proficiency and comprehension skills

You can assess your performance and predict your scores using the free AIDLA MDCAT/ECAT Calculator.

👁 0 views🏛️ Admissions
Was this helpful?

MDCAT aggregate for government medical colleges is calculated by adding 50% of FSC marks and 50% of MDCAT score.

To calculate the aggregate, you need to know your FSC percentage and MDCAT score. The FSC marks are first converted to a percentage, then 50% of this percentage is added to 50% of your MDCAT score.

  • FSC weightage: 50% of FSC marks are included in the aggregate calculation
  • MDCAT weightage: 50% of MDCAT score is included in the aggregate calculation
  • Merit calculation: The aggregate score determines your merit position for government medical college admissions

You can calculate your exact MDCAT aggregate using the free AIDLA MDCAT/ECAT Calculator.

👁 0 views🏛️ Admissions
Was this helpful?

Open merit seats in Pakistani universities are allocated based on a student's academic performance, with admission offered solely on merit.

In contrast, self-finance seats are available to students who can afford to pay higher tuition fees, often because they did not meet the merit criteria or because the university has a limited number of open merit seats.

  • Eligibility: Open merit seats require a high academic score, while self-finance seats have more flexible eligibility criteria
  • Fees: Self-finance seats typically have higher tuition fees compared to open merit seats
  • Admission process: Students can apply for both open merit and self-finance seats, but the admission process and requirements may differ

You can assess your academic performance and potential eligibility for open merit or self-finance seats using the free AIDLA Percentage Calculator.

👁 0 views🏛️ Admissions
Was this helpful?

MDCAT passing score is 180 out of 200, with a minimum 60% score required for medical colleges in Pakistan.

The MDCAT exam is a crucial step for admission to medical colleges, with each college having its own merit criteria. Your MDCAT score, along with your FSC grades, determines your overall merit.

  • Merit calculation: Typically, 50% weightage is given to MDCAT scores and 30-40% to FSC grades
  • Competitive scores: Scores above 190 are generally considered competitive for top medical colleges
  • College-specific merit: Each college releases its own merit list after the MDCAT results

You can estimate your MDCAT score and merit using the free AIDLA MDCAT/ECAT Calculator.

👁 0 views🏛️ Admissions
Was this helpful?

ECAT (Engineering Colleges Admission Test) is a standardized test for admission to engineering and computer science programs in Pakistani universities.

In Pakistan, ECAT is mandatory for admission to public and private sector engineering universities, with each institution having its own merit criteria and weightage for ECAT scores. You should check the specific requirements for your desired university.

  • Key subjects: ECAT tests your knowledge in subjects like physics, chemistry, mathematics, and English.
  • Top universities: Many top engineering universities in Pakistan, such as UET Lahore and NED University, accept ECAT scores for admission.
  • Score calculation: You can calculate your expected ECAT score using the free AIDLA MDCAT/ECAT Calculator.
  • Admission criteria: ECAT scores are often combined with intermediate exam results to determine the final merit list.

You can find more information about ECAT and university admissions on the AIDLA Resources page.

👁 0 views🏛️ Admissions
Was this helpful?
✈️

Study Abroad

2 questions

SAT preparation in Pakistan involves focused studying, typically 3-6 months prior to the exam, covering math, reading, and writing sections.

To prepare, you should familiarize yourself with the exam format, practice with official study materials, and consider enrolling in a preparation course or working with a tutor. Creating a study plan and setting realistic goals is also essential.

  • Official study materials: Utilize the Official SAT Study Guide for comprehensive preparation
  • Practice tests: Take regular practice tests to assess your performance and identify areas for improvement
  • Time management: Develop a study schedule to ensure consistent progress and minimize stress

You can create a personalized study plan using the free AIDLA Study Planner to help you stay on track and achieve your SAT goals.

👁 0 views✈️ Study Abroad
Was this helpful?

Preparing for the IELTS exam in Pakistan from scratch involves mastering English reading, writing, listening, and speaking skills.

To begin, you should familiarize yourself with the IELTS format, question types, and assessment criteria. Focus on improving your vocabulary, grammar, and pronunciation by practicing with sample questions and exercises.

  • Language skills: Enhance your reading, writing, listening, and speaking abilities through regular practice
  • Study materials: Utilize official IELTS study guides, online resources, and practice tests to prepare
  • Time management: Create a study plan and set realistic goals to ensure consistent progress

You can create a personalized study plan using the free AIDLA Study Planner to help you stay on track and achieve your desired IELTS score.

👁 0 views✈️ Study Abroad
Was this helpful?
💻

Technology

3 questions

Artificial intelligence is revolutionizing education in Pakistan by enhancing learning experiences through personalized adaptive systems.

In Pakistan, AI is being used to develop intelligent tutoring systems, automate grading, and provide real-time feedback to students. This technology is also helping to increase access to quality education, particularly in rural areas.

  • Personalized learning: AI-powered systems can tailor educational content to individual students' needs
  • Intelligent tutoring: AI can provide one-on-one support to students, supplementing traditional teaching methods
  • Automated assessments: AI can help teachers grade assignments and exams more efficiently and accurately

You can explore AI-powered tools, such as the AIDLA AI Summarizer, to enhance your learning experience.

👁 0 views💻 Technology
Was this helpful?

Learning programming from scratch in Pakistan involves starting with basic concepts and gradually moving to advanced topics, focusing on popular programming languages like Python, Java, or JavaScript.

To begin, you can enroll in online courses or coding boot camps that cater to Pakistani students, providing structured learning paths and mentorship. Practicing coding exercises and working on personal projects helps solidify your understanding of programming concepts.

  • Online resources: Utilize platforms offering free coding lessons and exercises
  • Join communities: Participate in Pakistani coding communities for support and feedback
  • Build projects: Apply your skills by developing real-world projects

You can enhance your learning experience and track progress using the AIDLA Study Planner.

👁 1 views💻 Technology
Was this helpful?

The best free online courses for Pakistani students include technology and programming courses on platforms like Coursera, edX, and Udemy.

These courses cover a wide range of topics, such as data science, artificial intelligence, and cybersecurity, and are often taught by industry experts.

  • Popular courses: Data structures, algorithms, and web development are in high demand
  • Skills enhancement: Free online courses can help improve your programming skills and knowledge
  • Career opportunities: Having a strong online presence and relevant skills can increase your chances of getting hired

You can find more resources and tools, including the AIDLA Courses and Resources to help you get started with your online learning journey.

👁 0 views💻 Technology
Was this helpful?
🏛

CSS & PMS

6 questions

CSS exam in Pakistan allows six attempts for each candidate.

The Central Superior Services exam is highly competitive, and candidates often require multiple attempts to succeed. You should carefully plan and prepare for each attempt to achieve the best possible results.

  • Eligibility criteria: Candidates must meet the eligibility criteria set by the Federal Public Service Commission
  • Age limit: The age limit for CSS exam candidates is 21-30 years, with some exceptions for certain categories
  • Exam preparation: Effective preparation is crucial to succeed in the CSS exam, and utilizing resources like the AIDLA Resources can be beneficial

You can find more information and study materials on the AIDLA website to help you prepare for the CSS exam.

👁 2 views🏛 CSS & PMS
Was this helpful?

The NTS test is a standardized entrance exam required for various government jobs in Pakistan, particularly for positions in the public sector.

In Pakistan, the National Testing Service (NTS) administers tests for recruitment in government departments, educational institutions, and other public sector organizations. These tests assess candidates' knowledge, skills, and abilities in areas such as general knowledge, English, and subject-specific knowledge.

  • Typical subjects: NTS tests cover a range of subjects, including English, mathematics, and general knowledge
  • Government jobs: Many government departments, such as the Federal Public Service Commission and the Provincial Public Service Commissions, require NTS test scores for recruitment
  • Preparation: Candidates can prepare for the NTS test using study materials and online resources, including the AIDLA Resources section

You can find more information on NTS test requirements and preparation materials on the AIDLA Courses page.

👁 1 views🏛 CSS & PMS
Was this helpful?

PMS Punjab exam preparation involves a comprehensive study plan, focusing on subjects like English, Urdu, General Knowledge, and optional papers.

To begin, familiarize yourself with the exam syllabus and pattern, and create a study schedule to manage your time effectively. Practice with past papers and mock tests to assess your knowledge and identify areas for improvement.

  • Understand the syllabus: Know the exam format and content
  • Develop a study plan: Allocate time for each subject and topic
  • Practice consistently: Use past papers and mock tests to track progress

You can utilize the AIDLA Study Planner to organize your study schedule and stay on track.

👁 0 views🏛 CSS & PMS
Was this helpful?

Cracking the FPSC exam requires a thorough understanding of the federal public service commission's exam pattern and syllabus.

To increase your chances of success, focus on developing a strong grasp of current affairs, general knowledge, and subject-specific knowledge. Create a study plan and stick to it, allocating sufficient time for each subject.

  • Subject knowledge: Focus on key subjects like English, Urdu, and general knowledge
  • Practice tests: Regularly practice past papers and mock tests to assess your performance
  • Time management: Develop a strategy to manage your time effectively during the exam
  • Revision: Regularly review and revise your notes to reinforce your learning

You can create a personalized study plan using the free AIDLA Study Planner to help you stay organized and focused.

👁 0 views🏛 CSS & PMS
Was this helpful?

CSS (Central Superior Services) and PMS (Provincial Management Services) exams are two competitive exams in Pakistan, with CSS being a federal-level exam and PMS being a provincial-level exam.

While both exams are used to recruit candidates for government services, they differ in their scope, syllabus, and requirements. CSS is conducted by the Federal Public Service Commission (FPSC) for federal government jobs, whereas PMS is conducted by the provincial public service commissions for provincial government jobs.

  • Exam scope: CSS has a broader scope, covering federal government jobs across Pakistan
  • Syllabus: PMS exams have a more focused syllabus, tailored to the specific needs of each province
  • Eligibility: Both exams require a bachelor's degree, but CSS has more stringent eligibility criteria

You can prepare for these exams using the free resources available on the AIDLA Resources page, and track your progress with the help of the AIDLA Study Planner.

👁 0 views🏛 CSS & PMS
Was this helpful?

The age limit for the CSS exam in Pakistan is 21-30 years for general candidates and 21-32 years for those from certain categories.

Candidates must have a bachelor's degree with at least 2nd division from a recognized university to be eligible for the CSS exam.

  • Age relaxation: 2 years of age relaxation for categories such as Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and government servants
  • Academic requirement: A bachelor's degree with at least 2nd division from a recognized university is required
  • Citizenship: Candidates must be citizens of Pakistan or Azad Kashmir

You can prepare for the CSS exam by creating a study plan using the free AIDLA Study Planner.

👁 0 views🏛 CSS & PMS
Was this helpful?
💬
